context: =  tumor > 10 mm in size Incidence:  10% (= 70-80% of pituitary adenomas) Age:     25-60 years; M÷F = 1÷1 √  occasionally tumor hemorrhage √  lucent areas ← cysts / focal necrosis √  invasion of cavernous sinus → encasement of carotid artery (SUREST sign) CT: √  tumor isodense to brain tissue √  erosion of bone (eg, floor of sella) √  calcifications infrequent MR: (allows differentiation from aneurysm) √  homogeneous enhancement Cx: DDx:
